author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
Why the Apostle Said that We are Justified by Faith and Not by Works.
title: Why the Apostle Said that We are Justified by Faith and Not by Works.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Chapter 12 [VII.]—Why the Apostle Said that We are Justified by Faith and Not by Works.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But perhaps it may be said: “The apostle distinguishes faith from works; he says, indeed, that grace is not of works, but he does not say that it is not of faith.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But Jesus says that faith itself also is the work of God, and commands us to work it.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For the Jews said to Him, “What shall we do that we may work the work of God?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Jesus answered, and said unto them, This is the work of God, that ye believe on Him whom He hath sent.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: The apostle, therefore, distinguishes faith from works, just as Judah is distinguished from Israel in the two kingdoms of the Hebrews, although Judah is Israel itself.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And he says that a man is justified by faith and not by works, because faith itself is first given, from which may be obtained other things which are specially characterized as works, in which a man may live righteously.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For he himself also says, “By grace ye are saved through faith; and this not of yourselves; but it is the gift of God,”—that is to say, “And in saying ‘through faith,’ even faith itself is not of yourselves, but is God’s gift.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: “Not of works,” he says, “lest any man should be lifted up.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For it is often said, “He deserved to believe, because he was a good man even before he believed.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Which may be said of Cornelius since his alms were accepted and his prayers heard before he had believed on Christ; and yet without some faith he neither gave alms nor prayed.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For how did he call on him on whom he had not believed?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But if he could have been saved without the faith of Christ the Apostle Peter would not have been sent as an architect to build him up; although, “Except the Lord build the house, they labour in vain who build it.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And we are told, Faith is of ourselves; other things which pertain to works of righteousness are of the Lord; as if faith did not belong to the building,—as if, I say, the foundation did not belong to the building.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But if this primarily and especially belongs to it, he labours in vain who seeks to build up the faith by preaching, unless the Lord in His mercy builds it up from within.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Whatever, therefore, of good works Cornelius performed, as well before he believed in Christ as when he believed and after he had believed, are all to be ascribed to God, lest, perchance any man be lifted up.
